code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into fluent, combined sentences."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    from collections import defaultdict

    # Group objects by subject and predicate (allow multiple values per predicate)
    subj_info: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(list))
    for t in triples:
        subj_info[t.subject][t.predicate].append(t.object)

    sentences: list[str] = []

    # Helper functions for common predicates
    def list_join(items: list[str]) -> str:
        if not items:
            return ""
        if len(items) == 1:
            return items[0]
        return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + " and " + items[-1]

    # Mapping for single‑value predicates to phrasing functions
    single_map = {
        "birthDate": lambda o: f"was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda o: f"is {o} meters tall",
        "weight": lambda o: f"weighs {o}",
        "position": lambda o: f"plays as a {o}",
        "league": lambda o: f"competes in the {o}",
        "manager": lambda o: f"is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda o: f"is coached by {o}",
        "owner": lambda o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda o: f"has {o} as general manager",
        "chairman": lambda o: f"has {o} as chairman",
        "draftYear": lambda o: f"was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda o: f"was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda o: f"was pick number {o.strip('\"')}",
        "playerNumber": lambda o: f"wears number {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda o: f"currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda o: f"debuted with {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda o: f"formerly played for {o}",
        "club": lambda o: f"plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda o: f"plays for {o}",
        "leader": lambda o: f"is led by {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda o: f"died in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f"is in {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "ground": lambda o: f"has home ground {o}",
        "stadium": lambda o: f"has stadium {o}",
        "season": lambda o: f"participated in the {o} season",
        "currency": lambda o: f"uses currency {o}",
        "demonym": lambda o: f"has demonym {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"has official language {o}",
        "language": lambda o: f"{o} is spoken",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda o: f"includes ethnic group {o}",
        "anthem": lambda o: f"is the anthem {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda o: f"has most championships {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"has leader title {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda o: f"has UTC offset {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda o: f"is in time zone {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda o: f"covers area {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda o: f"was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Mapping for predicates that can have multiple values
    multi_map = {
        "playerNumber": list_join,
        "club": list_join,
        "currentteam": list_join,
        "formerTeam": list_join,
        "currentclub": list_join,
    }

    # Build sentences for each subject
    for subject, predicates in subj_info.items():
        parts: list[str] = []
        # Begin with the subject name
        subject_phrase = subject
        # Process single‑value predicates first
        for pred, func in single_map.items():
            if pred in predicates:
                # Use the first value for single‑value predicates
                obj = predicates[pred][0]
                parts.append(f"{subject_phrase} {func(obj)}")
                # Remove to avoid double handling
                del predicates[pred]

        # Process multi‑value predicates
        for pred, func in multi_map.items():
            if pred in predicates:
                objs = predicates[pred]
                parts.append(f"{subject_phrase} {func(objs)}")
                del predicates[pred]

        # Any remaining predicates are added generically
        for pred, objs in predicates.items():
            for obj in objs:
                parts.append(f"{subject_phrase} has {pred} {obj}")

        # Combine parts for this subject
        if parts:
            sentences.append(". ".join(parts) + ".")

    # Combine all subject sentences
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
